commit.message:
Pixi support (#2)

* Fix missing submodule for UCE (#15)

* Fix bug in preprocessing of sparse vs non-sparse adata  (#14)

* Simplify installation instructions in README

Removed redundant instruction for downloading the repository.

* Remove unnecessary extra environment for gseapy access (covered by main env)

* Update setup.sh to comment flash-attn installation

Comment out flash-attn installation due to environment issues.

* Add pixi and nix flake configuration for local development

Add pixi.toml with conda-forge dependencies (Python 3.10, PyTorch, scanpy,
geneformer, etc.) and pip editable install of cellwhisperer + cellxgene fork.
Include flake.nix providing a NixOS FHS environment to run pixi.

* Update cellxgene submodule: Werkzeug 3.x fix, pandas 2.x, rename to cellxgene-cellwhisperer

Points to epigen/cellxgene commit 90345e3 which includes:
- Fix FlatBuffers bytes/bytearray compatibility with Werkzeug 3.x
- Require pandas>=2.0.1
- Rename package to cellxgene-cellwhisperer to avoid conflicts
- Skip Puppeteer download in client Makefile

* Fix torch.load compatibility with PyTorch 2.6+

PyTorch 2.6 changed torch.load default to weights_only=True, which rejects
checkpoints containing custom classes (e.g. TranscriptomeTextDualEncoderConfig).
Pass weights_only=False explicitly where needed, and monkey-patch torch.load in
load_from_checkpoint to handle Lightning checkpoint loading.

* Make scgpt imports lazy to support environments without scgpt

Move scgpt imports inside functions that use them so the package can run with
geneformer (default) without requiring scgpt to be installed. Also use string
comparison for foundation model class checks to avoid import dependency.

* Add pixi installation instructions to README

Add pixi as the top-most (recommended) installation option, keeping the
existing conda instructions as Option B.

* Fix missing gseapy in pixi

* Replace local LLaVA/Mixtral with API + lightweight local curation

- llava_annotate_clusters now uses the hosted CellWhisperer API instead of
  requiring a local LLaVA model and separate conda env
- Unified curate_llava_annotations rule replaces separate gpt4/mixtral rules:
  uses OpenAI GPT-4 if OPENAI_API_KEY is valid, falls back to local
  Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct via transformers
- Add cellwhisperer_api_url to config.yaml (used by API annotation rule)
- Upgrade transformers>=4.45 and add accelerate for local model support
- Add gseapy dependency

* Retain local LLaVA annotation rule as llava_annotate_clusters_local

API-based rule is preferred via ruleorder. The local rule can be
invoked explicitly with --forcerun llava_annotate_clusters_local
and requires the llava conda env + GPU.

* Update README with pixi commands and new curation fallback docs

* Add news section to landing page (Pixi support, Mila talk)

* Revert "Add news section to landing page (Pixi support, Mila talk)"

This reverts commit 76f405e4d7a21f793a1993e96c6d96cf4d97cfbd.

* Fix checkpoint loading for newer transformers and add frontend build step to README

* Simplify Pixi setup: remove Nix files, drop uni dep, add setup script (#25)

* Initial plan

* Address review comments: remove flake files, remove uni dep, simplify README with setup script

feat: upload status report video to Azure

I implemented the step of uploading the status report video after it has
been recorded and approved to Azure Blob Storage. I created an Azure
Function that will create a short-lived SAS URL for the client
application to upload the video directly to Blob Storage. The iOS
application uploads the video to the SAS URL in the background, allowing
the upload to continue even if the app is suspended. I also added error
handling and user feedback for the upload process.

fix: include plugin-core classes on runtime classpath for buildSearchableOptions

IPP 2.x buildSearchableOptions creates its own fresh sandbox separate from
the prepareSandbox output. The doLast hack that copies plugin-core-classes.jar
into the regular sandbox is not visible to buildSearchableOptions, so any
configurable or service that references plugin-core types fails with
ClassNotFoundException / "Can't instantiate configurable".

Fix: add runtimeOnly(files(repackagePluginCore)) after the repackagePluginCore
task declaration in both standalone-mcp and plugin-experimental. This puts the
repackaged JAR (no plugin.xml) on the runtime configuration, which IPP uses
when setting up the buildSearchableOptions classpath — so ToolPermissionsConfigurable
and all other plugin-core-backed configurables can be loaded without error.

The existing doLast copy in prepareSandbox is kept as belt-and-suspenders for
the regular sandbox (used by runIde / deploy-to-ide).

commit.message:
feat(mcp,a2a): MCP gateway and A2A protocol support (#139)

* feat(a2a): add bitrouter-a2a crate with A2A v1.0 client and agent identity

Implement the A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol for BitRouter, enabling agent
discovery, identity management, and communication with remote A2A servers.

The bitrouter-a2a crate provides:
- Full A2A v1.0 type coverage: AgentCard, Task, Message, Part, Artifact,
  SecurityScheme, and all lifecycle states
- JSON-RPC 2.0 wire format types for the A2A transport
- A2A protocol client (discover, message/send, tasks/get, tasks/cancel)
- File-based agent card registry with DNS label name validation
- Warp HTTP filters for /.well-known/agent-card.json discovery

The bitrouter binary gains a unified `bitrouter a2a` CLI:
- Local identity: register, list, show, rm agent cards
- Remote communication: discover, send, status, cancel tasks

Also adds RequestContext.agent_name for per-agent observability, mounts
A2A discovery endpoints on the server, and includes a Claude Code skill
for agent-to-agent communication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert: undo unintended changes to crates outside bitrouter-a2a and bitrouter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(api): move A2A Warp filters from bitrouter-a2a to bitrouter-api

HTTP filters belong in bitrouter-api (layer 4) per the project's layered
architecture. This moves well_known_filter and agent_list_filter behind a
feature-gated `a2a` module, consistent with existing provider patterns.
Removes the warp dependency from bitrouter-a2a.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(a2a): align wire format with A2A v1.0 specification

Update all JSON serialization to match the A2A v1.0 wire format:
- MessageRole: "user"/"agent" → "ROLE_USER"/"ROLE_AGENT"
- TaskState: "submitted" etc → "TASK_STATE_SUBMITTED" etc
- Part: tagged enum with "kind" → flat struct with text/data/raw/url
- JSON-RPC methods: message/send → SendMessage, tasks/get → GetTask,
  tasks/cancel → CancelTask
- AgentCard structs: add camelCase serde renaming for interop
- SendMessage result: handle both Task and Message responses
- Artifact: add description field

Tested end-to-end against a2a-go helloworld server on localhost:9001.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(a2a): add A2A v1.0 server with JSON-RPC dispatch and LLM executor

Adds server-side A2A support so BitRouter can accept incoming A2A
requests and execute them via its LLM routing system. This enables
two BitRouter instances to communicate via A2A on behalf of their
agent runtimes (e.g. Claude Code, OpenClaw).

New traits: AgentExecutor, TaskStore (bitrouter-a2a)
New filter: POST /a2a JSON-RPC dispatch (bitrouter-api)
New impls: LlmAgentExecutor, InMemoryTaskStore (bitrouter binary)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(a2a): implement full A2A v1.0 spec compliance with interop-tested wire format

Add all 11 A2A v1.0 JSON-RPC methods, typed request/response structs,
streaming (SSE), REST bindings, push notification CRUD, and protocol
binding enum. Wire format validated against a2a-go reference implementation:
StreamResponse uses externally-tagged enums ({"task":{...}}), SSE events
wrapped in JSON-RPC envelopes, and unified /a2a endpoint handles both
streaming and non-streaming methods.

New modules: request.rs (typed params), stream.rs (SSE event types),
push_store.rs (in-memory push notification store).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(a2a): restructure A2A modules into focused submodules

Split the monolithic A2A handler/filters into four focused submodules
(discovery, jsonrpc, streaming, rest) matching the OpenAI adapter pattern.
Move FileAgentCardRegistry from bitrouter-a2a to bitrouter runtime where
other concrete implementations live. Group A2A runtime files into a2a/
subdirectory for consistency with the API-side restructuring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(mcp): add MCP gateway with multi-upstream tool aggregation

Introduce bitrouter-mcp crate with protocol types, config, tool
filtering, parameter restrictions, access groups, and error types.
Wire the MCP gateway into the runtime behind the `mcp` feature flag:
upstream connections (stdio + HTTP), namespaced tool aggregation,
runtime filter/param mutation via admin API, tool spend observation,
and CLI subcommands for tools management. Extend JWT claims and
identity with tool-name access patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(mcp): add bitrouter-mcp crate README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(mcp): add MCP server mode with JSON-RPC/SSE transport

Enable bitrouter to serve as an MCP server to downstream clients via
POST /mcp (JSON-RPC) and GET /mcp/sse (notifications). The server
exposes aggregated tools from all configured upstream MCP servers and
propagates tool_list_changed notifications downstream.

Architecture follows Option 3: own wire types in bitrouter-mcp (no rmcp
dependency), warp filters in bitrouter-api, rmcp stays binary-only for
the client side.

- bitrouter-mcp/src/server/: JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope types, MCP protocol
  messages (initialize, tools/list, tools/call, ping), McpTool types,
  error codes, and McpToolServer trait
- bitrouter-api/src/router/mcp/server.rs: warp filter builder with
  JSON-RPC dispatch and SSE notification stream

commit.message:
Implement JWT + refresh token authentication system

- Add RefreshToken model to Prisma schema (family-based rotation detection)
- Create tokens.ts: createRefreshToken, rotateRefreshToken, revokeRefreshToken,
  revokeAllUserTokens, cleanupExpiredTokens with SHA-256 hashed storage
- Update auth.ts: 15-min access tokens with automatic refresh token rotation
  in the JWT callback; refresh tokens issued on login (credentials + Google)
- Add /api/auth/refresh endpoint for manual token rotation (mobile/API use)
- Create SessionGuard component: auto-logout on RefreshTokenExpired error
- Update SessionProvider with refetchInterval (14min) and refetchOnWindowFocus
- Add session expired banner on login page (?expired=1)
- Revoke all tokens on user ban/admin-removal (admin users endpoint)
- Add token cleanup to admin clear-caches action
- Security: token reuse detection revokes entire token family (theft protection)

Token lifecycle:
  Access token: 15 minutes (auto-refreshed silently)
  Refresh token: 30 days (rotated on each use, revoked on sign-out)
  Session container: 30 days (NextAuth maxAge)

commit.message:
fix: replace deprecated ReadAction.compute/run with ApplicationManager.runReadAction

Replace all usages of deprecated ReadAction.compute() and ReadAction.run()
with ApplicationManager.getApplication().runReadAction() across plugin-core
and plugin-experimental. These APIs are deprecated in IntelliJ 2026.1 (IU-261).

Also replace deprecated JBPopupFactory.createListPopupBuilder(JBList) with
createPopupChooserBuilder(List) in ChatToolWindowContent.kt, using the new
Consumer<T> callback instead of Runnable.

Document intentional use of experimental/scheduled-for-removal APIs in
PlatformApiCompat where no non-experimental alternatives exist:
- LafManager.getInstalledThemes() (@ApiStatus.Experimental, no alternative)
- DataPack via DataPackChangeListener (scheduled-for-removal, but using the
  internal DATA_PACK_REFRESH alternative would cause a build FAILURE)

feat: implement M10 and M11 milestone features

M10 — Enhanced Chat & Conversational AI:
- M10-1: Chat session branching (fork/switch/delete branches)
- M10-2: Conversation templates (8 built-in: code_review, explain_code, write_tests, write_docs, debug_error, optimize_code, security_audit, refactor_suggestion)

- M10-4: Chat message bookmarks & pinning with persistence
- M10-5: Multi-modal input (image context via base64)
- M10-6: Smart context switching (active file context per project)
- M10-7: Real-time streaming via SSE (StreamingResponse, simulated chunking)
- M10-8: Message threading (threaded replies to specific messages)
- M10-9: Chat analytics dashboard (messages, personas, feedback, bookmarks, branches, threads)
- M10-10: Conversation summarization (compress old history, retrieve summary)

M11 — Advanced AI Intelligence:
- M11-1: Multi-model routing (keyword-based rule engine with custom rules)
- M11-2: AI agents marketplace (6 built-in specialist agents + install endpoint)
- M11-3: Code generation from requirements
- M11-4: Cross-project knowledge graph (scan Python/JS/TS/C# files, extract symbols)
- M11-5: AI-powered test intelligence (generate tests + coverage hints)
- M11-6: Semantic code search (index files, LLM-ranked results)
- M11-7: Adaptive persona learning (feedback log + LLM-suggested prompt improvements)
- M11-8: AI pair programming mode (start/analyze/stop/status)

Also marks M10 and M11 milestones and all their tasks as 'done' in roadmap.json.

security: add explicit null byte rejection to PathJail.Validate

PathJail.Validate relied on Go 1.21+ syscall-layer null byte rejection,
which made the test non-deterministic. Add explicit strings.ContainsRune
check for null bytes before any filesystem operation (CWE-158).
